title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: Findings: - High: [assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh:119](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L119) masks shell-task failures with `|| true`, then captures `$?` on the forced-success path at [line 121](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L121). Any failing manual command in shell mode will be reported as success, so this pane cannot be trusted for debugging. - Medium: [assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh:79](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L79), [line 90](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L90), and [line 116](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L116) hard-depend on `gtimeout` with no fallback. On a stock macOS setup that binary is usually absent, so the new CI/CD pane will fail before running tests/builds/tasks. - Medium: [assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md:45](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md#L45) still cleans up `Tester-Codex`, but the pane is now renamed to `CI/CD+Debug` at [line 60](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md#L60). Re-running the setup will leave stale CI/CD panes behind. - Medium: [assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md:75](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md#L75), [line 84](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md#L84), and [line 125](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/canon/SKILL.md#L125) still describe `$RIGHT/$MID/$BOT` and `Tester-Codex`. The launch contract and operator instructions are now inconsistent with the actual pane names/variables, which will misroute later commands. - Medium: [assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh:347](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L347) through [line 413](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L413) removed the old CJK-text skip from bash-block parsing. Japanese explanatory lines inside fenced shell examples will now be interpreted as commands and penalized as phantom commands, which is a regression for this repo’s docs. I did not flag the `experience.jsonl` churn; it looks like generated data rather than logic.
